channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ghana conducts voluntary repatriation of citizens amid rising xenophobic tensions in South Africa
item.description: On Wednesday, almost 300 Ghanaian nationals voluntarily returned to Ghana from South Africa, in what migration expert Loren Landau describes as an unprecedented development in post-apartheid South Afr...

item.title: NACC criticised for inconsistent approach in corruption investigations
item.description: The National Anti-Corruption Commission (NACC) has come under scrutiny for apparent inconsistencies in its investigative methods. In its recent report on 'Operation Young', the NACC found an Australia...

item.title: Taxpayers’ body urges Andhra Pradesh to remove additional VAT and fuel cess to ease fuel prices
item.description: The Taxpayers’ Association, Vijayawada, has urged the Andhra Pradesh government to withdraw the additional Value Added Tax (VAT) and the road development cess imposed on petrol and diesel, stating tha...

item.title: High Court suspends planned US-linked Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya pending hearing
item.description: The High Court in Nairobi has temporarily halted plans to establish a United States-linked Ebola quarantine and treatment facility in Kenya, pending a full hearing of a case challenging the arrangemen...

item.title: The Quad’s future amid US politics and Indo-Pacific tensions
item.description: The article examines the current state and future relevance of the Quad — the strategic grouping of the United States, Australia, Japan and India — amid shifting geopolitical dynamics and the second T...

item.title: Boat Capsizes in River Ganga in Bihar's Samastipur District, Three Dead and Four Missing
item.description: In a tragic incident on Thursday morning, a small boat carrying 14 passengers capsized in the mighty River Ganga in Bihar's Samastipur district. The accident occurred around 5:45 AM near Umanath Ghat ...

item.title: Supreme Court Advises NTA to Strengthen Security After NEET-UG 2026 Paper Leak
item.description: On May 3, 2026, the NEET-UG medical entrance exam faced a major setback due to a paper leak, prompting its cancellation and the scheduling of a re-test on June 21. The Supreme Court, while hearing pet...

item.title: Senator Jinggoy Estrada to Post Bail Over Graft Allegations in Flood Control Projects
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada is set to post bail after the Sandiganbayan issued an arrest warrant against him for alleged graft related to kickbacks in flood control projects. Estrada emphasized that he is...

item.title: Clarification issued on earlier report regarding COVID-19 vaccine myths and expert statements in India
item.description: The article refers to an editorial clarification published by The Times of India concerning a previously published report on COVID-19 vaccine myths in India. The original 2020 article titled 'How vacc...

item.title: Kasarani MP Ronald Karauri Discusses His Leadership Journey and Plans for Nairobi
item.description: Kasarani Member of Parliament Ronald Karauri has opened up about his transition from aviation and business into politics, saying Kenya needs more leaders than politicians to foster genuine development...

item.title: Governor Fubara Highlights Peace, Unity, and Inclusive Development in Rivers State Anniversary
item.description: Rivers State Governor, Sir Siminalayi Fubara, don reaffirm say him government dey committed to maintaining peace, unity, security, and inclusive development as the state celebrate 59 years since e cre...

item.title: Woman killed outside Sheffield bar as two charged with murder and related offences
item.description: Two people have been charged in connection with the death of Shanice Brookes, a 30-year-old charity worker who was fatally injured outside a Sheffield bar. Police said she was an innocent bystander on...

item.title: Strand man walks again after bilateral osseointegration surgery
item.description: A 46-year-old Strand resident, Tashinga Skepa, has regained the ability to walk independently following a complex bilateral limb reconstruction procedure performed at Tygerberg Hospital. Skepa sustain...

item.title: Nova Scotia Artist and Biologist Team Up for Environmental Activism
item.description: In Nova Scotia, a world-renowned artist and his lifelong friend, a biologist, have combined their passions to become active environmental advocates. Their journey began with a shared love of the outdo...

item.title: The Aardwolf: A Shy, Termite-Eating Relative of the Hyena
item.description: The article introduces the aardwolf, a nocturnal African mammal belonging to the hyena family, but with a highly specialized and unusual diet. Unlike its more aggressive hyena relatives that scavenge ...

item.title: Nigeria’s investors increase interest in corporate debt markets
item.description: For years now, na government securities like Treasury bills and sovereign bonds dey dominate the Nigerian fixed-income market. Dem dey seen as safe, liquid, and dem dey give better yields, so e dey ma...

item.title: Trans Nzoia Governor George Natembeya unveils new county assembly headquarters building
item.description: Trans Nzoia Governor George Natembeya has unveiled the newly completed county assembly headquarters, marking a key milestone in the county’s infrastructure development agenda. The modern facility, who...

item.title: Asda partners with Ocado to upgrade its online grocery and delivery systems from 2027
item.description: Asda, the UK’s third-largest supermarket, has announced a major strategic partnership with retail technology company Ocado to significantly improve its online grocery operations and home delivery serv...

item.title: Simon-Pierre Savard-Tremblay resigns from Bloc Québécois to pursue Parti Québécois candidacy
item.description: Simon-Pierre Savard-Tremblay, the Bloc Québécois Member of Parliament for Montreal, has officially left the party’s caucus to seek election with the Parti Québécois in Quebec provincial politics. His ...

item.title: U.S. Black Hawk Helicopter Aids in Intercepting Drug Smuggling Vessel Carrying 391 Pounds of Cocaine Near Puerto Rico
item.description: On May 14, U.S. Customs and Border Protection's Air and Marine Operations deployed a Black Hawk helicopter to assist in the interception of a 25-foot blue vessel suspected of drug smuggling off the co...
